content: You're not the only one. This has been an ongoing issue with Thunderbird and Google Calendar for years. Local calendars work fine. Maybe other on-line calendar services. But Google... nope. It seems to be a permissions issue on Google's side that nobody has been able to surmount, as far as I know. There have been a number of suggested fixes and workarounds, none of them I have found to work. The only solution I have found has been to disable notifications for that calendar (uncheck the "Show Reminders" option from that calendar's right-click Properties context menu.)
creator: rednoise

content:
I don't know where you got the idea that Thunderbird and Google are collaborating against you. It's not true. Also, reinstalling Thunderbird rarely solves problems like this because all specific email account information is stored in your Profile, which a reinstall doesn't touch.

I advice would be to:

- Make sure cookies are fully allowed in Thunderbird;

- Remove all existing cookies for Gmail;

- remove all stored passwords for that Gmail account;

- restart Thunderbird. You should then be prompted for your credentials, and hopefully Gmail will like them.

I won't go into a lot of detail right now because it sounds like you have already made up your mind, but if you would like to try to work through this, please let us know.
creator: rednoise

content: Hover with the mouse over the vertical divider line of the Subject column. This will turn the mouse pointer into a double-headed arrow. You can now drag the line to adjust the width of the column to your liking.

content:
DSGraham a écrit
The spacing of the Subject column label is inconsistent and apparently not under the control of a user. In the graphic with yellow notations, the leading space for Subject is much larger than any of the other column labels shown.

The spacing between the vertical line (separating folder pane and thread pane) and the Subject column label depends on whether you sort your messages by threaded or unthreaded: go to View > Sort by > Threaded/Unthreaded When you sort them by "Threaded" the spacing is greater because of the additional expand/collaps icons being displayed before the subjects (see image)

content: Find a file called " persdict.dat" in your Profile folder. This is a list of words you have added to your personal dictionary. It's a simple text file list of words you have added, one word per line. It can be viewed and edited with any plain text editor (NOT a word processor.) Be sure to close down Thunderbird first.
creator: rednoise

content: Or you could use the edit button when checking the spelling of a document.

content:
Between the two suggestions above, I was able to work out this answer: C:\Users\jmrhi\AppData\Roaming\Thunderbird\Profiles\l3s7y169.default-release\persdict.dat where jmrhi is my username.

When asked, I selected Notepad as the app in which to view that file.

Thanks!

content:
SMTP (Outgoing Mail Server) HOSTNAME smtp-mail.outlook.com

The correct outgoing server is smtp.office365.com. Manually adjust your settings. Authentication type must be OAuth2 for both, incoming, and outgoing server.

Also make sure cookies are allowed in Thunderbird. Do not use a VPN while attempting to authenticate. If you do run a local web server, shut it down while attempting to authenticate.

Also see https://support.mozilla.org/kb/microsoft-oauth-authentication-and-thunderbird-202
creator: christ1

content:
After adding the German dictionary, Thunderbird froze

Are you using the German version of Thunderbird? If so, the German dictionary is already built-in. There is no need to add it (again).

after adding ImportExport Tools, Thunderbird not only crashed but also wouldn’t start up again.

Try to start TB in troubleshooting mode. https://support.mozilla.org/kb/troubleshoot-mode-thunderbird

If it starts properly, the problem is likely with an add-on. ImportExportTools NG works fine here with TB 150.0. https://addons.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/addon/importexporttools-ng/
